data.title: Viking Holdings trades higher after Rothschild points to long-term upside
data.description:
Viking Holdings Ltd. (VIK) moved higher in early trading on Tuesday after Rothschild & Co. Redburn upgraded the cruise line stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Neutral.

Analyst Alex Brignall and his team see further upside for the stock despite the near-term concerns over fuel prices. The analyst team noted that on a normalized free cash flow basis, Viking's (VIK) multiple is close to those of Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) and Carnival (CCL) and at a significant discount to that of Royal Caribbean (RCL).

Looking ahead, Rothschild sees Viking (VIK) benefitting from "healthy secular demand drivers, best-in-class revenue visibility, and strong cash generation."

Shares of Viking (VIK) were up 2.2% in mid-morning trading. The cruise line stock has a consensus Buy rating with both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts.

data.title: Credo surges after deal to acquire photonics company
data.description:
Shares of Credo Technology (CRDO) surged about 13% on Tuesday after the company announced postmarket on Monday that it is acquiring DustPhotonics in a cash and stock deal.

DustPhotonics develops Silicon Photonics Photonic Integrated Circuit, or SiPho PIC, technology for optical transceivers.

Credo will pay $750M cash upfront and about 0.92M common shares. The company may pay incremental contingent consideration of up to about 3.21M shares based on achieving certain milestones.

Credo said the acquisition accelerates its optical interconnect roadmap and significantly expands its served addressable market in the global optical industry. These SiPho PICs are deployed in transceivers at leading hyperscale AI clusters and are also in design for leading Near Port Optics ((NPO)) and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) applications.

The company noted DustPhotonics' SiPho PICs are deployed in transceivers at leading hyperscale AI clusters and are also in design for leading Near Port Optics, or NPO, and Co-Packaged Optics, or CPO, applications. 

"This acquisition is encouraging as it supports vertical integration of Credo’s optical transceiver offering. Additionally, we believe this acquisition enables Credo to broaden its scale-up networking opportunity to SiPh-based CPO/NPO solutions. Because we already forecast in FY27 that Credo will generate $200M in optical DSP [Digital Signal Processors] sales and $100M in ZF optical transceiver sales, we think Credo’s outlook implies that DustPhotonics could generate at least $200M in sales in FY27. Without considering revenue and expenses, DustPhotonics will need to generate at least ~$20M in net income to be accretive to Credo’s FY27 non-GAAP earnings," said BNP Paribas' analysts David O’Connor and Karl Ackerman.

Related stocks: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) slumped about 4%, while Astera Labs (ALAB) and Ciena (CIEN) each fell nearly 3%. Lumentum (LITE) declined around 2%, while Coherent (COHR) dipped about 1%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) seemed to buck the trend and climbed about 2% on Tuesday.

Update: The story was updated with a BNP note and stock price movement.

data.title: S&P 500 returns to positive territory and erases Middle East conflict losses
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) has staged a strong comeback, returning to positive territory for the year and fully recovering losses tied to the recent U.S.-Iran conflict in the Middle East. The benchmark index is now trading at 6,922, up 1.5% on the session and 0.6% higher year to date.

This marks a notable turnaround from its recent low of 6,317.41, recorded on March 30, from which the index has rallied roughly 9.5%. The recovery underscores renewed investor confidence and resilient market momentum despite geopolitical uncertainties.

Importantly, the S&P 500 now sits just 1% below its all-time high of just over 7,000, which it reached on January 28, signaling that equities are approaching record levels once again. 

The sharp rebound has been driven by strength across key sectors, with top-performing stocks leading the advance and helping fuel the broader market’s recovery.

Outlined below are the top 20 S&P 500 performing stocks of 2026: 

  1. Sandisk (SNDK), +295.5% YTD
  2. Lumentum (LITE), +138.75% YTD
  3. Ciena (CIEN), +105.74% YTD 
  4. Western Digital (WDC), +101.49% YTD

data.title: Omega Healthcare cut to Sell-equivalent at BoA Securities
data.description:
Omega Healthcare Investors (OHI) was trading lower as BoA Securities cut its recommendation on the stock amid competition and regulatory risks.

The stock was downgraded to Underperform from Neutral, with the price target reduced to $46 from $52. OHI was 0.81% down to $45.05 Tuesday pre-market.

"With total return potential of 7.2%, OHI screens as an Underperform within our U.S. REIT coverage. Reflecting heightened competitive dynamics, regulatory pressures, and asset quality considerations relative to peers, we are reducing our FY26 AFFO estimate in line with the Street and lowering our AFFO multiple to 15.0x from 16.5x," said analysts Farrell Granath, Jeffrey Spector, and Julieta Michelin.

The research note said an upside to earnings is contingent on accretive external growth execution and continued improvement in tenant credit quality.

"As WELL, SBRA, and CTRE remain active SNF (skilled nursing facility) acquirers, OHI appears relatively disadvantaged in a more competitive market. Its larger market cap requires materially sized deals to impact earnings at increasingly competitive prices," said BofA analysts.

"Thus, we are cutting our FY26 acquisition volume assumption to $633M from $929M, reducing AFFO/share estimate ~80 basis points," said the note.

"While the subsector (SNF) benefits from favorable demographic tailwinds, it faces elevated regulatory and public reimbursement risk," said the note.

"Proposed tighter regulation, potential limits on REIT ownership, and a lower FY27 SNF PPS initial update of +2.4% (from +3.2% in FY26) could pressure tenant coverage despite stable fundamentals resulting in downward pressure on multiples," said the analysts.

BoA's rating differs from the sell-side analysts, Seeking Alpha authors, and Quant ratings of Buy.

data.title: Ford rallies after UBS says investors are missing the upside potential
data.description:
UBS upgraded Ford Motor (F) to a Buy rating after having the Detroit auto stock set at Hold. The firm's main contention is that the company's earnings power is being underappreciated by investors.

Analyst Joseph Spak and his team see a credible path to Ford (F) earning more than $2 in EPS in 2027. "Beyond 2027, we see F embarking on a march towards $3 in EPS power driven by product portfolio, a more lenient U.S. regulatory backdrop combined with a more pragmatic EV strategy, an emerging battery energy storage system opportunity, and more focus on higher margin Pro software. Shares have been under pressure given recent concerns over higher gasoline prices and higher aluminum prices, both of which we believe are overdone, with aluminum prices not impacting 2026 as they are hedged," he highlighted.

Crucially, USB thinks the market is pricing in Ford (F) 2027 EPS of $1.73, which is about 16% below the firm's forecast, and noted to be underappreciating the transient headwinds expected to dissipate in the second half of this year and the 2027 EPS power.

Shares of Ford (F) broke 4.4% higher in the first half hour of trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Small-cap communication services stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the communication services sector enters reporting season navigating a complex mix of tailwinds and headwinds.

Within the small-cap segment of communication services, upward EPS revisions are a particularly meaningful signal that analyst sentiment is turning constructive ahead of Q1 reports. 

The following list highlights small-cap communication services stocks that have earned a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A- or higher, spanning integrated telecommunications, advertising technology, interactive media, and publishing, with market capitalizations ranging from approximately $300 million to $1.25 billion, and four companies, including ATN International and IDT Corporation, earning the highest possible A+ grade.

The list represents a variety of industries within the communication services sector, including integrated telecommunications, publishing, advertising, and interactive media. While the top four stocks hold A+ grades, the list remains competitive with several other companies like Angel Studios, Inc. (ANGX), Anterix Inc. (ATEX), and EverQuote, Inc. (EVER) maintaining solid A grades, and others such as Bumble Inc. (BMBL) and MediaAlpha, Inc. (MAX) earning A- grades.

data.title: Performance Shipping to sell Aframax Vessel M/T P. Aliki for $42.6M
data.description:
Performance Shipping (PSHG) has signed a memorandum of agreement to sell its oldest vessel, the 2010-built Aframax tanker vessel, M/T P. Aliki, to Trafigura Maritime Logistics for $42.65M.

The company acquired the M/T P. Aliki in the fourth quarter of 2022 for a gross purchase price of $36.5M. The vessel currently serves as part of the collateral for the company’s outstanding credit facility with Alpha Bank S.A.

Part of the net proceeds from the sale will be applied to repay ~$12.8M in accordance with the terms of the loan agreement.

The vessel is expected to be delivered to the new owners in or around the end of the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and upon expiration of her current US$30,000 per day time charter with Pakistan National Shipping.

data.title: IonQ surges after getting DARPA contract
data.description:
IonQ (IONQ) said it has been awarded a contract in the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency's (DARPA) Heterogeneous Architectures for Quantum (HARQ) program.

Shares of the quantum computing company surged about 10% on Tuesday.

The company noted that the HARQ contract will support IonQ's development of high-speed interconnects capable of linking multiple types of quantum computers.

As a part of HARQ, IonQ plays a key role in enabling a new class of networked quantum computers that combine distinct qubit types — such as trapped ions, neutral atoms, and/or superconducting qubits — into an interconnected, high-performance architecture, according to the company.

IonQ said that this effort seeks to use advances in photonic integration and quantum interconnects for reliable communication between diverse qubit species.

"We look forward to collaborating with DARPA to strengthen national security by developing the quantum platform which can serve as a backbone for networking and scaling quantum systems - across qubit types - for advanced public and private sector applications," said IonQ's Chairman and CEO Niccolo de Masi.

DARPA is an independent research and development agency within the U.S. Department of War.

data.title: Crowded trades shift to oil and semiconductors as gold loses top spot
data.description:
Investors are crowding into oil and semiconductor trades, according to Bank of America’s latest global fund manager survey, underscoring how positioning is converging around a narrow set of macro and technology themes.

A net 24% of respondents identified “long oil” as the most crowded trade, while an equal share pointed to “long global semiconductors,” leaving the two tied at the top in April. The shift highlights continued conviction in energy markets alongside persistent enthusiasm for chip-related exposure tied to structural demand trends.

The positioning marks a notable change from the previous month, when “long gold” and “long global semiconductors” dominated as the most crowded trades, each cited by 35% of investors. The drop in gold’s prominence suggests a rotation away from traditional defensive plays even as geopolitical risks remain elevated.

Beyond the top trades, the survey shows a steep fall-off in crowding elsewhere. “Long gold” now ranks third at 15%, followed by “long Magnificent 7” at 11%. On the bearish side, smaller shares of investors flagged “short global consumer discretionary” and “short private credit” as crowded positions, both at 9%.

The results point to increasingly concentrated positioning across asset classes, with investors leaning into a mix of commodity exposure and technology leadership themes rather than broad diversification.

Bank of America said the findings are based on its April survey of global fund managers.

data.title: Gas price shock will only partly offset tax refunds - JPMorgan
data.description:
Surging gasoline (UNG) costs induced by the war in Iran are eating into the financial lift that U.S. households are getting from bigger income tax refunds this year, though most taxpayers will still come out ahead, according to a new analysis from JPMorgan Asset Management.

The firm’s research shows that every income group retains some degree of residual stimulus after accounting for higher fuel expenses, but the cushion varies dramatically depending on where households fall on the income ladder—painting a lopsided picture for consumers. 

Middle-income households appear to fare the worst in relative terms. For those in the 40%–60% and 60%–80% income brackets, higher gasoline prices absorb nearly all the benefit from larger tax refunds. Lower-income households end up slightly better off, but only modestly so.

The biggest winners are at the top. JPMorgan’s estimates indicate that the top 20% of earners receive the largest refund increases by a wide margin. Even after factoring in elevated fuel costs, they retain the strongest positive financial impulse of any group.
